texto: It was never a good idea to laugh at a God as powerful as Hades. Although, the sight of him with cream-colored paint smeared in his raven dark hair and smudged on his nose was comical. "Already went that route. Me bedroom ended up a nasty shade of chartreuse. Took me a whole week to do it again." Hades sniffed, rubbed his nose, and looked at the paint on his fingers. "Damn it. It's in my hair, too, isn't it?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Casey Wyatt que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “It was never a good idea to laugh at a God as powerful as Hades. Although, the sight of him with …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, hades, paranormal-romance, com foco em termino e reflexao-noturna.
